evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC for 4 classifiers with AUROC/AP legend annotations, diagonal and prevalence reference lines — fully matches brief.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 never set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (non-selectable) fonts; font family is Times New Roman (good) and sizes are sensible (9 pt base, 7.5 pt legend).
evidence.palette: CB-safe four-color set (gray/teal/blue/coral); no green or red used for directional meaning; OursModel coral stands out against receding gray Baseline.
evidence.layout_density: 6.75×2.9 in figure with tight_layout; axis limits -0.02–1.02/1.05 leave minimal dead space; legends placed in natural empty corners.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Panel is double-labeled: ax.set_title starts with '(a)' AND a separate ax.text adds '(a)' — Reviewer 2 would flag redundancy; caption omits actual AUROC/AP numbers.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows Type 3 Custom-encoded fonts throughout; fonttype=42 not set in rcParams or savefig call, so text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (32 KB), figure.png (178 KB), caption.tex (539 B), gen_figure.py (4.4 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Makes a specific directional claim ('OursModel achieves the highest AUROC and AP') and explains both reference lines, but provides no actual numerical AUROC/AP values in the caption body.

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe palette with clear visual hierarchy — OursModel coral stands out, Baseline recedes in gray, and distinct linestyles add a redundant encoding channel.

All required output files are present and non-trivial; the script correctly computes curves from raw scores and fully satisfies the brief's two-panel layout with reference lines and per-method metric annotations.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is absent, producing Type 3 (bitmap-encoded) fonts in the PDF — text is not selectable or editable, which fails the vector-fidelity requirement for publication submission.

Panel labels are duplicated (axis title string starts with '(a)'/'(b)' and a separate ax.text also draws them), and the caption never quotes actual AUROC/AP numbers, weakening its standalone informativeness.
